name: Cream of Eggplant Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Dice the eggplant but do not peel it. Sprinkle a little salt over the eggplant and leave to stand for about 30 minutes. Rinse in cold water briefly, then pat dry. This removes any bitter taste which would spoil the soup.
2. Melt the butter in a saucepan and add the onion, celery, and eggplant. Saute', stirring occasionally until the vegetables have slightly softened. Eggplant absorbs butter very rapidly; you may need to add a little more butter or the vegetables will stick.
3. Add the curry powder and fry until the flavors are released.
4. Add thyme, basil, chicken stock, and potato. Cover and simmer until the vegetables are quite soft, about 30 minutes.
5. Transfer the soup through a sieve or food mill into a bowl, reserving the liquid. Stir in the cream. Taste for seasoning. If desired, garnish with diced tomato or chopped chives for color.
This soup keeps well for several days. However, if making it beforehand and keeping it, only add the cream when reheating. It thickens as it stands but can be thinned with a little milk.
